title: Room visual identity — distinct tile themes per room type
problem: All room types (combat, chest, shrine, boss, exit) render with the same floor/wall tile colors. Players cannot visually distinguish room types at a glance — the room grammar exists in data but is invisible in the UI. Boss rooms, shrines, and exits should have immediately recognizable visual signatures.

lineage.spawnReason: deferred polish: per-room-type visual theming was explicitly cut from the initial room grammar implementation

analysis: Charnel (combat-heavy) and Sanctum (shrine/chest-heavy) rooms were structurally distinct but looked identical on the grid. Players had no visual hint about room atmosphere before stepping in. A subtle floor tint costs nothing render-wise and communicates room type passively.
implementation: In src/screens/GridScreen.tsx, updated the bgColor else-branch (plain visible floor tiles not yet encountered and with no encounter type). Added a getRoomTypeAt call to get the room type for the current tile coords, then applied a dark red tint (#100808) for Charnel rooms and a dark purple tint (#07060f) for Sanctum rooms. Standard floor color applies to neutral rooms. Fog-edge bleed (FOG_EDGE_COLOR) still takes priority over the room tint for tiles adjacent to fog.

decisions.decision: Room tints are very dark — #100808 red and #07060f purple — barely distinguishable from the base floor color
decisions.rationale: The hint should be subliminal; a bright red floor would be distracting and feel out of place in a dungeon tile set.

decisions.decision: Fog-edge bleed (FOG_EDGE_COLOR) takes priority over room tint
decisions.rationale: Fog-edge bleed is a visibility aid; overriding it with the room tint would break the existing fog visual.

decisions.decision: getRoomTypeAt is called per-tile in render, not cached
decisions.rationale: getRoomTypeAt is a pure O(rooms) lookup; rooms array is tiny (< 10 elements). Per-tile call is negligible.

successCriteria: Charnel room floor tiles have a faint red tint vs standard floor tiles. Sanctum room floor tiles have a faint purple tint. Fog-edge tiles still show FOG_EDGE_COLOR. Tile color changes don't affect encounter type overlays (those override bgColor earlier in the chain).
knownLimitations: Tints are very subtle — colorblind players on small screens may not notice them
Visited (encountered) tiles revert to VISITED_FLOOR color, losing the room tint — by design to avoid visual noise on explored areas
userGuide: Charnel rooms (monster-heavy) have a faint red floor tint. Sanctum rooms (shrine/chest-heavy) have a faint purple floor tint. These are subtle visual hints — you will feel it more than see it.
implementationNotes: Only unencountered plain floor tiles get the room tint — walls, exits, and visited tiles are unaffected
getRoomTypeAt is imported from engine/grid; rooms and roomTypes come from useGridStore

codeSnippets.label: Room tint in bgColor logic
